   - Configuration errors
   - Skipped or xfail tests

4. **Summarize test results**:
   - Total tests run
   - Passed / Failed / Skipped / Error counts
   - List each failure with its category and brief description


- Run the actual tests and capture real output
- If tests take too long, use a timeout and report partial results; never present targeted-only PR-mode tests as a full-suite pass
- In PR mode, `targeted` test scope is opt-in. `full` (the default) means the user expects comprehensive verification — do NOT silently downgrade to targeted because the suite is large; instead report the skipped command honestly
- Always post your findings as a GitHub comment before completing

===== agentic_checkup_step7_verify_LLM.prompt =====
% You are an expert software engineer performing a health checkup on a project. Your task is to verify fixes and generate a final report.

% Context

You are working on step 7 of 8 in an agentic checkup workflow. Previous steps have discovered the project, audited it, and optionally applied fixes.

% Iteration Info

This is fix-verify iteration {fix_verify_iteration} of {max_fix_verify_iterations}.
If issues remain after this verification, the workflow may loop back to steps 3-6 for another fix attempt.

% Inputs

- GitHub Issue URL: {issue_url}
- Repository: {repo_owner}/{repo_name}
- Issue Number: {issue_number}
- Project Root: {project_root}
- Report-only mode (no fixes): {no_fix}
- PR-verification mode: {pr_mode}
- PR URL (PR mode only): {pr_url}
- PR Number (PR mode only): {pr_number}
- PR Repo (PR mode only): {pr_owner}/{pr_repo}
- PR test scope (PR mode only): {pr_test_scope}
- PR changed files (PR mode + targeted scope only):
<pr_changed_files>
{pr_changed_files}
</pr_changed_files>

% User Request
<issue_content>
{issue_content}
</issue_content>

% Previous Steps Output
<step1_output>
{step1_output}
</step1_output>

<step2_output>
{step2_output}
</step2_output>

<step3_output>
{step3_output}
</step3_output>

<step4_output>
{step4_output}
</step4_output>

<step5_output>
{step5_output}
</step5_output>

<step6_1_output>
{step6_1_output}
</step6_1_output>

<step6_2_output>
{step6_2_output}
</step6_2_output>

<step6_3_output>
{step6_3_output}
</step6_3_output>

<pr_push_output>
{pr_push_output}
</pr_push_output>

% Your Task

1. **Re-run the build** to confirm no build errors remain
   - Use the same build commands from Step 3
   - Capture the full output

2. **Re-run tests appropriate for the mode**:
   - In issue mode, re-run the full discovered suite: unit, integration, and e2e tests, including any new regression/e2e tests from Steps 6a-6c.
   - In PR mode with `PR test scope` = `full` (the default), re-run the full discovered suite plus any regression/e2e tests created in Steps 6a-6c. PR mode does NOT imply targeted scope; the user opted into the comprehensive quality gate by not passing `--test-scope targeted`. If the full suite is too large to complete within this step's budget, report the exact full-suite command as not run due to time budget and set `"success": false` with a partial-verification finding — do not silently fall back to targeted tests.
   - In PR mode with `PR test scope` = `targeted`, re-run the PR-scoped tests tied to `<pr_changed_files>`, plus any regression/e2e tests created in Steps 6a-6c, and any targeted build or contract tests needed to verify issue alignment. The user explicitly opted into targeted scope; do NOT run the full suite.
   - If PR mode is true, test scope is `targeted`, and `<pr_changed_files>` says PR changed files are unavailable, do not broaden into a timeout-prone full-suite run on a large repository. Re-run only cheap build/static checks, regression/e2e tests created in Steps 6a-6c, and any small tests clearly tied to the issue/prior failures; report that concrete PR scope could not be established and do not claim full-suite or complete PR coverage.
   - If `<pr_changed_files>` includes a full API changed-file list artifact path, read that local file before selecting PR-scoped verification tests; the inline list may be a bounded preview.
   - Capture the full output for every command you do run.

3. **Compare results** with previous steps:
   - Which previously failing tests now pass?
   - Are there any new failures introduced by the fixes?
   - Do all regression and e2e tests from Steps 6b/6c pass?
   - Are there any remaining build errors?

4. **Frame the verdict by mode** — this is the question Step 7 actually answers:

   - **Issue mode** (default; `pr_mode` = `false`): "Did the fixes applied in Steps 6.1–6.3 resolve the issue described in `<issue_content>`, with no new regressions?" Set `success` true if yes, false otherwise.

   - **PR mode** (`pr_mode` = `true`): "Does the current PR worktree for `{pr_url}` actually resolve the issue at `{issue_url}`?" In PR mode the workflow runs inside the PR worktree (NOT the issue-author's HEAD). When fix mode is enabled, Steps 6.1-6.3 may have applied local checkup fixes that are not pushed until after Step 7. Your verdict must evaluate the current worktree state, including those local fixes, because this is the exact tree that will be committed and pushed back to the same PR if verification passes. If `<pr_push_output>` says the push rebased onto an updated PR head, this is a post-push verification pass and you must verify that final rebased worktree head. You MUST surface a top-level boolean `issue_aligned` in the JSON: `true` when the PR worktree's changes substantively address the issue, `false` when the PR is unrelated, tests-only-without-real-fix, or otherwise misaligned with the reported defect. Treat `issue_aligned: false` as a higher-severity signal than any individual finding — downstream cloud consumers can gate terminal-fail on this field.

5. **Generate the final JSON report**

The JSON report MUST be the LAST thing you output, on its own, wrapped in a markdown code block:

```json
{{
  "success": true/false,
  "message": "summary of findings and actions taken",
  "tech_stack": ["language1", "framework1", ...],
  "issue_aligned": true/false,
  "issues": [
    {{
      "module": "module_name",
      "file": "path/to/file",
      "severity": "critical|medium|low",
      "category": "missing_dep|import_error|build_error|test_failure|interface_mismatch|artifact",
      "description": "what was wrong",
      "fixed": true/false,
      "fix_description": "what was done to fix it (or empty if not fixed)"
    }}
  ],
  "changed_files": ["list", "of", "modified", "files"]
}}
```

In **issue mode**, omit `issue_aligned` (or set it to `null`) — it only applies when verifying an existing PR.
In **PR mode**, `issue_aligned` is REQUIRED.

Set `"success": true` if (issue mode) all critical issues are fixed, or (PR mode) `issue_aligned` is true AND no critical findings remain in the PR's diff. In PR mode with `PR test scope` = `full`, do not set `"success": true` if the full suite was skipped or timed out; either cite an authoritative full-suite/CI result or set `"success": false` with a partial-verification finding. In PR mode with `PR test scope` = `targeted`, the final report MUST explicitly label verification as targeted in the markdown summary and the JSON `message` (e.g., "Verification scope: targeted — full suite not run") so downstream consumers do not mistake targeted coverage for full-suite coverage.
Set `"success": false` otherwise.

6. **Prepare the final summary** — route by mode:

   - **Issue mode** (`pr_mode` = `false`): post to the issue thread only.
     ```
     gh issue comment {issue_number} --repo {repo_owner}/{repo_name} --body "..."
     ```

   - **PR mode** (`pr_mode` = `true`): do NOT post GitHub comments from
     Step 7. In full PR mode, this verification pass runs before generated
     fixes are pushed to the PR branch, so a pre-push comment can be falsely
     clean if the later push fails. Output the markdown summary and final JSON
     only; the orchestrator posts the final PR/issue report after the pushed
     PR head has been verified.

Your markdown summary should follow this format:

```markdown
## Step 7/8: Verification & Final Report (Iteration {fix_verify_iteration})

### Test Results After Fixes
- **Total:** [N] tests
- **Passed:** [N]
- **Failed:** [N]
- **Previously failing, now passing:** [N]
- **New failures:** [N]

### Build Status
[Pass/Fail — details if fail]

### Overall Status
[All clear / N issues remain]

### Verification Scope
[PR mode only — state explicitly: "full suite" or "targeted (full suite not run)"]

### Issue Alignment
[PR mode only — omit in issue mode]
[issue_aligned: true/false — and one sentence explaining whether the PR addresses the issue's intent]

### Issues Summary
| Severity | Category | Module | Description | Fixed |
|----------|----------|--------|-------------|-------|
| critical | missing_dep | auth | Missing requests | Yes |

---
*Checkup complete.*
```

% Important

- The JSON report MUST be the last `{...}` object in your output — this is parsed programmatically by walking the text from start to end and taking the LAST successfully-parsed JSON object. Intermediate reasoning blocks (fenced or unfenced) that contain `{...}` MUST NOT appear after the final verdict, or the parser will mistake them for the verdict.
- Always re-run tests even in --no-fix mode to confirm the baseline
- In PR mode, targeted tests are acceptable for time-bounded evidence ONLY when the user opted in via `PR test scope` = `targeted`. The final report must explicitly label them as targeted and must not imply that the full suite passed unless it actually ran or an authoritative CI/full-suite result is available. When `PR test scope` = `full` (the default), targeted-only verification is not acceptable as a basis for `"success": true`.
- Report ALL issues found across all steps, whether fixed or not
- In issue mode, post your findings as a GitHub comment before outputting the JSON
- In PR mode, do NOT post GitHub comments from Step 7; output the markdown summary and JSON only
- IMPORTANT: Use the EXACT iteration number ({fix_verify_iteration}) in your comment header — do NOT hardcode "Iteration 1"
- **Exit signal**: If the required verification for the active mode passes AND the build succeeds with no remaining issues, you MUST include the exact text "All Issues Fixed" in your output. In issue mode, required verification means ALL tests pass (unit + integration + e2e). In PR mode, required verification means PR-scoped tests plus any required regression/e2e/contract tests pass, and either the full suite/CI is clean or the report truthfully states that verification was targeted only. If any required test fails, build issues remain, or full-suite evidence is required but missing, do NOT output "All Issues Fixed" — instead clearly list remaining issues so the next iteration can fix them


===== agentic_checkup_step8_create_pr_LLM.prompt =====
% You are an expert software engineer. Your task is to create a pull request from the worktree branch with all checkup fixes and link it to the original GitHub issue.

% Context

You are working on step 8 of 8 (final step) in an agentic checkup workflow. All fixes have been applied and verified in previous steps. Now you create a PR for review.

% Inputs

- GitHub Issue URL: {issue_url}
- Repository: {repo_owner}/{repo_name}
- Issue Number: {issue_number}
- Issue Title: {issue_title}
- Worktree Path: {worktree_path}
- Branch Name: checkup/issue-{issue_number}
- Files Changed: {files_to_stage}

% Issue Content
<issue_content>
{issue_content}
</issue_content>

% Fix Summary (from Step 6a)
<step6_1_output>
{step6_1_output}
</step6_1_output>

% Verification Results (from Step 7)
<step7_output>
{step7_output}
</step7_output>

% Your Task

1. **Stage all changes**
   ```bash
   cd {worktree_path}
   git add -A
   ```

2. **Create commit**
   - Use a clear commit message referencing the issue
   - Format: `fix: checkup fixes for #{issue_number}`

3. **Push branch**
   ```bash
   git push -u origin checkup/issue-{issue_number}
   ```

4. **Create pull request**
   Use `gh pr create` to create a PR:
   ```bash
   gh pr create --title "fix: Checkup fixes for #{issue_number}" --body "..." --base main --head checkup/issue-{issue_number}
   ```

5. **Link PR to issue**
   - The PR body should include `Fixes #{issue_number}`

6. **Post final comment to issue**

% PR Body Format

```markdown
## Summary

Automated checkup fixes for #{issue_number}.

Fixes #{issue_number}

## Changes Made

[List of fixes applied from Step 6 output]

## Verification

[Summary of test results from Step 7 output]

## Review Checklist

- [ ] Fixes are correct
- [ ] Regression tests included
- [ ] All tests pass

---
*Created by pdd checkup workflow*
```

% Issue Comment Format

Post a comment on the issue using:
```
gh issue comment {issue_number} --repo {repo_owner}/{repo_name} --body "..."
```

```markdown
## Step 8/8: Pull Request Created

**PR:** [#{{pr_number}}]({{pr_url}})

### Summary
- Branch: `checkup/issue-{issue_number}`
- Files changed: [count]

### What's Next
1. Review the PR
2. Run full test suite
3. Merge when satisfied

---
*Checkup workflow complete.*
```

% Important

- Always link the PR to the issue using "Fixes #N"
- Push to origin before creating PR
- Use `git add -A` to stage all changes in the worktree
- Do NOT create a new branch — you are already on `checkup/issue-{issue_number}` in the worktree
- This is the final step — workflow is complete after this


===== agentic_common_python.prompt =====
<pdd-reason>Provides shared agentic CLI execution across Claude, Gemini, Codex, and OpenCode.</pdd-reason>

<pdd-interface>
{
  "type": "module",
  "module": {
    "functions": [
      {"name": "get_agent_provider_preference", "signature": "() -> List[str]", "returns": "List[str]"},
      {"name": "get_available_agents", "signature": "() -> List[str]", "returns": "List[str]"},
      {"name": "run_agentic_task", "signature": "(instruction, cwd, *, verbose=False, quiet=False, label=\"\", timeout=None, max_retries=1, retry_delay=DEFAULT_RETRY_DELAY, deadline=None, use_playwright=False, reasoning_time=None) -> Tuple[bool, str, float, str]", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str, float, str]"},
      {"name": "detect_control_token", "signature": "(output, token, tail_lines=_SEMANTIC_TAIL_LINES) -> Optional[TokenMatch]", "returns": "Optional[TokenMatch]"},
      {"name": "classify_step_output", "signature": "(output, expected_tokens, model=\"gemini/gemini-3-flash\") -> Optional[TokenMatch]", "returns": "Optional[TokenMatch]"},
      {"name": "substitute_template_variables", "signature": "(template, context, *, strict_unresolved: bool = False) -> str", "returns": "str"},
      {"name": "set_agentic_progress", "signature": "(workflow, current_step, total_steps, step_name, completed_steps) -> None", "returns": "None"},
      {"name": "clear_agentic_progress", "signature": "() -> None", "returns": "None"},
      {"name": "get_and_clear_agentic_interrupt_context", "signature": "() -> Optional[Dict]", "returns": "Optional[Dict]"},

      {"name": "validate_cached_state", "signature": "(last_completed_step, step_outputs, step_order=None, quiet=False) -> Union[int, float]", "returns": "Union[int, float]"},
      {"name": "github_save_state", "signature": "(repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state: Dict, cwd: Path, comment_id: Optional[int] = None, *, dedupe: bool = False) -> Optional[int]", "returns": "Optional[int]"},
      {"name": "github_load_state", "signature": "(repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, cwd: Path) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[int]]", "returns": "Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[int]]"},
      {"name": "github_clear_state", "signature": "(repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, cwd: Path) -> bool", "returns": "bool"},
      {"name": "load_workflow_state", "signature": "(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[int]]", "returns": "Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[int]]"},
      {"name": "save_workflow_state", "signature": "(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state: Dict, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True, github_comment_id: Optional[int] = None, *, dedupe: bool = False) -> Optional[int]", "returns": "Optional[int]"},
      {"name": "clear_workflow_state", "signature": "(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True) -> None", "returns": "None"}
    ]
  }
}
</pdd-interface>

<pdd-dependency>llm_invoke_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>
<pdd-dependency>auth_service_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>
<pdd-dependency>_keyring_timeout_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>
<pdd-dependency>git_porcelain_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>
<include>context/python_preamble.prompt</include>

% Goal
Write the `pdd/agentic_common.py` module.

% Role & Scope
Shared infrastructure for agentic CLI invocations (Claude Code, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode). Provides reusable utilities for agent discovery, environment sanitization, logging, and subprocess orchestration. Acts as the low-level interface between PDD orchestrators and external agentic CLIs.

% External Documentation
<claude_code_json_output>
<web>https://claudelog.com/faqs/what-is-output-format-in-claude-code/</web>
</claude_code_json_output>

<gemini_cli_headless>
<web>https://geminicli.com/docs/cli/headless/</web>
</gemini_cli_headless>

<codex_cli_reference>
<web>https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli/reference</web>
</codex_cli_reference>

<opencode_cli_reference>
<web>https://opencode.ai/docs/cli</web>
</opencode_cli_reference>

<opencode_config_reference>
<web>https://opencode.ai/docs/config</web>
</opencode_config_reference>

<opencode_providers_reference>
<web>https://opencode.ai/docs/providers</web>
</opencode_providers_reference>

% Requirements
1. **Provider Preference**: Private `_DEFAULT_PROVIDER_PREFERENCE = ["anthropic", "google", "openai", "opencode"]`. Public `get_agent_provider_preference() -> List[str]` reads `PDD_AGENTIC_PROVIDER` (comma-sep) or default; `opencode` is a valid provider token.
2. **Logging**: `_log_agentic_interaction` writes to `.pdd/agentic-logs/session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.jsonl`. Issue #1376: every provider *attempt* — success, false-positive heuristic rejection, *and* every retry failure inside the retry loop — emits exactly one record so the log answers "which provider/model produced step N?" without `--verbose`. With `max_retries=N`, a fully failing single-provider run produces N rows (not 1). Each record carries `timestamp`, `label`, `cwd`, `provider`, `model`, `success`, `false_positive`, `cost_usd`, `duration_seconds`, `prompt_length`, `response_length`. The `model` field is resolved by `_extract_provider_model_from_data(provider, data)` first (keys of `modelUsage` for anthropic, keys of `stats.models` for google, `data["model"]`/nested for openai, and OpenCode JSON/JSONL model fields such as `model`, `session.model`, `message.model`, or `part.model` — multiple models join with `+`); when the JSON does not surface a model name (early-error returns, parse failures, or budget-skipped attempts), it falls back to `_get_provider_model(provider)` which reads `CLAUDE_MODEL` / `GEMINI_MODEL` / `CODEX_MODEL` / `OPENCODE_MODEL` env vars. `null` only when neither path yields a value. Full `prompt`/`response` bodies are gated by the keyword-only `include_bodies` parameter (default `True`). Call-site policy: failures and false-positives pass `include_bodies=True` (the bodies are the diagnostic payload — small responses with high signal); successes pass `include_bodies=verbose` (the prompt is large and identical across attempts, so summary-only by default keeps file size manageable). False-positive records pair `success=False` with `false_positive=True` so the run-level outcome stays correct while disambiguating heuristic rejection from CLI failure. Additionally, when a CLI exits 0 with empty/whitespace-only stdout, emit a diagnostic console line with stderr tail (last 500 chars), prompt size, sorted auth-env-key names (keys containing `TOKEN` or `API_KEY` with a truthy value), and cwd. This surfaces the blank-provider-error failure mode (cloud one-session sync) in batch logs.
3. **CLI Discovery**: Search via `_find_cli_binary`: `.pddrc` override (`agentic.{name}_path`) → `shutil.which` → `_COMMON_CLI_PATHS` (including `~/.npm-global/bin` for Node-installed CLIs, with nvm glob expansion `~/.nvm/versions/node/*/bin/`).
4. **Availability**: Anthropic (CLI exists); Google (CLI + `GOOGLE_API_KEY`/`GEMINI_API_KEY`/Vertex or stored Gemini CLI OAuth credentials in `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`); OpenAI/Codex (CLI + `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `PDD_CODEX_AUTH_AVAILABLE` or stored Codex login at `~/.codex/auth.json`); OpenCode (CLI + at least one usable OpenCode credential/provider signal). Codex CLI persists its ChatGPT/OAuth token in `~/.codex/auth.json` (created by `codex login`); detect it via `_has_codex_auth_file()` to keep runtime detection aligned with `pdd setup`'s `_has_provider_oauth("openai")` check (Issue #813 round-6) — without this, a user with only file-based Codex login finishes setup thinking Codex is configured, but `get_available_agents()` silently drops Codex from the runtime preference list. The availability check is still static and must not imply the Codex ChatGPT token can refresh successfully at execution time. Gemini CLI OAuth is a supported headless path; do not require Google API-key or Vertex env when the CLI has a stored OAuth token. For OpenCode, treat `opencode` as available when `_find_cli_binary("opencode")` succeeds and at least one of these is true: `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json` exists and parses to non-empty provider credentials; a discovered OpenCode config source (global `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, project `opencode.json` in or above `cwd`, `OPENCODE_CONFIG`, or `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT`) parses as JSON/JSONC and declares a usable selected `model`/`provider` pairing backed by a resolvable credential (`options.apiKey`, `{env:...}` with a set env var, `{file:...}` with a readable non-empty file) or an explicit local/provider configuration that does not require an API key; or any provider credential env var used by `pdd/data/llm_model.csv` is set, including pipe-delimited multi-var providers. Bare OpenCode config file existence, config content that only sets unrelated preferences, or config that references unset env/file substitutions MUST NOT make OpenCode available; keep that as diagnostic detail such as "OpenCode config found but no usable provider/model credential detected." Include providers such as Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini/Vertex, OpenRouter, GitHub Copilot (`GITHUB_TOKEN` when applicable), xAI, DeepSeek, Mistral-compatible entries, Cohere-compatible entries, Moonshot, Azure, AWS Bedrock, Ollama/LM Studio/local, and every other non-empty `api_key` value in the catalog. `OPENCODE_MODEL`, `OPENCODE_AGENT`, and `OPENCODE_VARIANT` are model/runtime knobs, not credentials, and MUST NOT by themselves make OpenCode available. Do not probe `opencode models` during availability detection.
5. **Execution**: Run in headless mode with JSON output. Sanitize env (TERM=dumb, NO_COLOR=1, CI=1). Strip `PDD_OUTPUT_COST_PATH`. Use `start_new_session=True` via `Popen` to kill process groups on timeout (Issue #830). **Anthropic auth hygiene** (Issue #813): under `CI=1` the `claude` CLI prefers `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` over its stored OAuth credential, so a stale or depleted key in the parent shell silently routes every call to the API tier and produces a confusing "Credit balance is too low" failure. Before launching the anthropic subprocess, conditionally pop `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` *only when* an OAuth credential is confirmed. Detection runs the documented `claude auth status` command first (lru-cached), with the env keys popped during the probe so a stale key can't shadow the answer; if that command exits nonzero or does not produce JSON, fall back once to `claude auth status --json` for CLI versions that still require the flag. Treat total probe failure (CLI missing, timeout, parse error for both command shapes, missing subcommand on older versions) as "no OAuth detected" and leave the key in place. An OAuth credential is present when the probe returns `loggedIn: true`, `authMethod` in {`claude.ai` (keychain Max/Pro), `oauth_token` (env-supplied `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` from cloud waterfall)}, and `apiProvider == "firstParty"` (Bedrock/Vertex routes are excluded by this last gate). Skip the pop entirely when `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK` or `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX` is set, when `PDD_KEEP_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set to a truthy value (`1`, `true`, `yes`, `on` — case-insensitive, with surrounding whitespace tolerated; explicitly disabling values like `0`, `false`, `no`, `off`, or empty string MUST be treated as not opting out so the fix still applies). When the strip happens, emit a one-shot dim notice ("Issue #813: dropped ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/AUTH_TOKEN ...") on the first invocation per process so users debugging "why did my API key stop being used?" can see it without `--verbose`; subsequent invocations stay silent unless `verbose=True`. The notice MUST be suppressed entirely when the caller passes `quiet=True` (orchestrator's "no non-error output" contract — scripted workflows redirect/parse stdout and a stray dim line breaks them); `quiet` takes precedence over `verbose` and does not consume the one-shot flag, so a later non-quiet caller still sees the notice once, or when neither env key is present (no-op fast path that avoids the probe cost). This conditional behavior preserves Issue #492's contract that explicitly-injected API keys (e.g. cloud GitHub App executor's Secret-Manager-injected `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on workers without OAuth) survive into the subprocess. **Codex/Gemini deliberately not covered by this scrub**: empirical testing shows the `codex` CLI does not exhibit the same shadowing under `CI=1` (it uses its `~/.codex/auth.json` token regardless of `OPENAI_API_KEY`); `gemini` shadowing is unverified. Extending the env-strip pattern to those branches without reproducing the symptom would create a new regression for users with only `GOOGLE_API_KEY`/`GEMINI_API_KEY`/`OPENAI_API_KEY` and no provider OAuth.
6. **Cost Parsing**:
   - Anthropic: `total_cost_usd` first; fallback to `modelUsage` sum; then token estimation.
   - Google: From `stats["models"]`.
   - OpenAI: From `usage`. Handle NDJSON (`item.completed`, `session.end`, `turn.completed`).
   - OpenCode: Parse JSONL events and sum `step_finish.part.cost` values first. If OpenCode does not report costs, fall back to the existing pricing/token-estimation path using the resolved OpenCode `provider/model` and matching `llm_model.csv` row.
7. **False Positives**: Treat empty output, (zero cost + short output < `MIN_VALID_OUTPUT_LENGTH`), or (cost > 0 + stripped output starts with `Error:` + newlines < `MAX_ERROR_RESPONSE_NEWLINES` + length < 4000) as failure. The `Error:` check must require the stripped output to *start* with `Error:` (a leading prefix of a genuine terse provider-error response) — never an unanchored substring match, which would demote substantive findings that merely *describe* error-raising code (Issue #1232). The newline-count gate (`MAX_ERROR_RESPONSE_NEWLINES = 3`) preserves the long-single-line error case from #902 while protecting multi-paragraph findings docs that happen to start with `Error:`. **Multi-provider configs** (`len(candidates) > 1`): `break` and fall through to the next provider — don't burn retries on a known-broken provider. **Single-provider configs** (`len(candidates) == 1`, e.g. cloud anthropic-only): no fallback exists, so retry on the same provider with exponential backoff (`retry_delay * 2 ** (attempt - 1) + random.uniform(0, retry_delay)`, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY`) up to `max_retries`, then break. An immediate `break` in single-provider configs yields zero retries on transient empty responses and surfaces as the blank-provider-error one-session-sync failure.
8. **Deadline Awareness**: Read `PDD_JOB_DEADLINE`. Skip attempts if remaining < 60s (after 120s reserve).
9. **GitHub State**: Persistence in issue comments via hidden markers (`<!-- PDD_WORKFLOW_STATE:... -->`). List comments with `gh api --paginate --slurp`, and flatten the resulting page arrays before scanning markers. Keep a parser fallback for older concatenated paginated JSON output so state loading and clean-restart sweeps still work if `--slurp` is unavailable or omitted by a future call site.
10. **State Validation**: `validate_cached_state` scans `step_outputs` for "FAILED:" to correct `last_completed_step`.
11. **PR Comments**: Provide `post_pr_comment(repo_owner, repo_name, pr_number, body, cwd) -> bool` that posts a comment to a PR via `gh pr comment {{pr_number}} --repo {repo_owner}/{repo_name} --body ...`. Handle missing `gh` CLI gracefully (return False). This is used by CI validation to report unresolved failures.
12. **Control Token Detection** (4-tier system for parsing workflow control tokens from LLM output):
   - `class TokenMatch`: Dataclass with `tier: str`, `token: Optional[str]`, `pattern: Optional[str]`, `cost: Optional[float]`. `tier` is one of "exact", "case_insensitive", "semantic", "llm_classification". Truthy via `__bool__ = True`.
   - `SEMANTIC_PATTERNS: Dict[str, List[str]]`: Regex patterns for each control token (ALL_TESTS_PASS, NOT_A_BUG, MAX_CYCLES_REACHED, CONTINUE_CYCLE, etc.). Patterns match paraphrased forms like "all N tests pass", "not a bug", "tests still failing".
   - `_SEMANTIC_TAIL_LINES = 30`: Only scan the last 30 lines for semantic patterns (prevents false positives from step output containing these phrases in quoted context).
   - `detect_control_token(output, token, tail_lines=30) -> Optional[TokenMatch]`: 3-tier detection: (1) exact substring, (2) case-insensitive, (3) semantic regex on tail. Returns `TokenMatch` with tier info or `None`. **Tier-3 line splitting must use `str.splitlines()` (portable line-boundary handling for `\n`, `\r\n`, and lone `\r`) — never `str.split('\n')`, which leaves trailing `\r` on each line and produces an extra empty element on CRLF input, shifting the `[-30:]` tail window by one and either excluding semantic phrases at the boundary (CRLF) or collapsing the whole output into one "line" and bypassing the tail restriction (lone `\r`). See Issue #1087.
   - `classify_step_output(output, expected_tokens, model="gemini/gemini-3-flash") -> Optional[TokenMatch]`: Tier-4 fallback — cheap LLM API call with structured JSON to classify output when tiers 1-3 fail. Only call after `detect_control_token` returns `None`. Returns a `TokenMatch` (whose `.token` carries the classified status, e.g. `ALL_TESTS_PASS`, or `CLASSIFICATION_ERROR` when the classifier itself fails) — callers dereference `.token` rather than treating the return as a bare string. Returns `None` only when the classifier confidently picks `NONE`.
13. **Retry with Exponential Backoff and Jitter**: `run_agentic_task` retries failed attempts with exponential backoff: `retry_delay * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) + random.uniform(0, retry_delay)`. This prevents thundering herd when concurrent jobs retry simultaneously. Cap maximum backoff at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY = 120`.
14. **Error Classification**: Classify errors from `_run_with_provider` as permanent or transient before deciding to retry:
   - **Permanent** (skip retries, move to next provider): authentication failure, invalid parameter, model not found, unsupported feature, quota exhaustion (TerminalQuotaError, "quota exhausted", "daily quota"), and billing/credit exhaustion. Match via `_is_permanent_error(error_msg) -> bool`. Anthropic-style billing failures surface as HTTP 400 bodies containing `credit balance is too low`, `plans & billing`, or `insufficient credit`/`insufficient balance`/`insufficient funds`; treat these as permanent so the orchestrator advances to the next provider in `PDD_AGENTIC_PROVIDER` order instead of burning all retries on the same provider (Issue #814). Classification runs in three tiers so an incidental 429/rate-limit token never masks a genuinely permanent failure: (a) **Strong-permanent patterns** — auth failures, invalid parameter/unsupported model, model not found, hard quota exhaustion (`quota exhausted/exceeded`, `daily quota`, `TerminalQuotaError`), credit exhaustion (`credit balance is too low`, `insufficient credit`/`balance`/`funds`), OAuth/login failures (`not logged in`, `please run /login`), and OpenCode misconfig — return `True` first. A `401 — see rate-limit docs` body still classifies as permanent so the orchestrator falls through to the next provider instead of sleeping on the 60s rate-limit floor (Issue #1072, Issue #814). (b) **Rate-limit short-circuit**: if no strong pattern matched and `_is_rate_limited(error_msg)` is `True`, return `False` so a generic HTTP 429 that just points the user at "Plans & Billing" keeps its standard retry/backoff treatment. (c) **Weak-permanent hint**: a bare `plans & billing` substring without any stronger signal is still treated as permanent so Anthropic billing pages without a 429 status are not retried (Issue #814). When a permanent classification fires, surface a one-line `console.print` diagnostic — visible in default non-verbose mode, suppressed under `quiet=True` to honor the quiet contract — naming the provider AND a stable classification token (one of: `auth`, `invalid-parameter`, `quota`, `billing/credit-exhaustion`, `oauth/login`, `provider-config`, `credential-limit`) returned by `_classify_permanent_error(error_msg) -> Optional[str]`. The `credential-limit` token matches Claude Code's subscription-tier `"You've hit your limit · resets <time>"` envelope (the matcher requires a time-token within ~40 chars after `resets` so descriptive prose like "if you hit your limit, nothing resets automatically" is not mis-classified). It is permanent so the loop does not burn rate-limit retries on a reset window measured in hours-to-days, and the token string is deliberately stable so pdd_cloud's OAuth waterfall can detect it and rotate credentials. The diagnostic line MUST NOT echo the raw provider stderr or any slice of it — that text is untrusted and can carry credentials a provider quoted back in its 401/auth body, so emitting it risks leaking keys to CI/stdout logs (Issue #814). The user can still recover the full provider output via `--verbose`, and every attempt is captured in the JSONL audit log regardless of mode. Because the diagnostic interpolates only a fixed classification string and the provider name, no Rich-markup escaping of untrusted input is required. OpenCode-specific permanent patterns include `provider not configured`, `model not found in provider`, `no provider configured for model`, and errors telling the user to configure a provider or run `opencode auth login`.
   - **Transient** (retry with backoff): rate limit (429), server error (5xx), timeout, network error.
   - This prevents wasting 3 retries on errors that won't resolve (e.g., temperature=1 rejection on Vertex AI).
   - Codex stale ChatGPT login failures are permanent and must be normalized before surfacing to users. If OpenAI/Codex exits nonzero and combined stderr/stdout includes Codex websocket/backend auth text such as `access token could not be refreshed`, `please sign in again`, `chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex`, `codex/responses`, plus `401`/`Unauthorized`/sign-in language, return a concise message telling the user to run `codex login` (or `codex login --device-auth`; `codex login --with-api-key` for API-key auth). Do not expose raw websocket URLs/log noise as the main failure. The recovery message MUST also tell the user how to skip Codex for the run: setting `PDD_AGENTIC_PROVIDER=anthropic,google` is the primary disable. Mention secondary options (unset `PDD_CODEX_AUTH_AVAILABLE` if set in env, or `codex logout` / remove `~/.codex/auth.json` since `_has_codex_auth_file` now picks up the file directly per Issue #813 round-6) so file-only Codex users have an actionable path — telling them to "unset `PDD_CODEX_AUTH_AVAILABLE`" alone is a no-op when their auth came from `~/.codex/auth.json` and that env var was never set.
15. **Aggregate Per-Step Timeout**: For local runs without `PDD_JOB_DEADLINE`, default to `2 * effective_timeout` as the step deadline. This caps worst-case to 2x the step timeout instead of 9x (3 providers x 3 retries).
16. **Progress Tracking** (Ctrl+C UX):
   - `set_agentic_progress(workflow, current_step, total_steps, step_name, completed_steps)`: Record current progress so KeyboardInterrupt can report how far the workflow got.
   - `clear_agentic_progress()`: Clear progress state.
   - `get_and_clear_agentic_interrupt_context() -> Optional[Dict]`: Retrieve and clear progress for Ctrl+C reporting.
17. **Template Variable Substitution**: `substitute_template_variables(template, variables) -> str`: Safe substitution that handles curly braces in LLM output without raising KeyError.
18. **Post Final Comment**: `post_final_comment(repo_owner, repo_name, issue_number, reason, total_cost, steps_completed, total_steps, cwd) -> bool`: Post a generated workflow summary comment to the GitHub issue when the workflow stops early. The function builds the comment body from the stop reason, cumulative cost, and completed/total step counts; callers do not pass a preformatted body.
19. **Trusted Step-Comment Helpers**: Orchestrators (not LLM step prompts) own visible per-step `## Step N/T:` comments on the GitHub issue, posted via `gh issue comment` with trusted PDD credentials. Step prompts emit a `<step_report>...</step_report>` block in their final response; the orchestrator extracts it, sanitizes/truncates, and posts once. Three helpers form the contract:
   - `extract_step_report(text: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]`: returns the trimmed contents of the LAST `<step_report>...</step_report>` block in `text` (DOTALL, case-insensitive). Returns `None` for empty input or when the block is missing/malformed. Missing or malformed report MUST NOT fail the workflow — orchestrators still post a short fallback visible comment, and the raw step output is preserved in `state["step_outputs"]` regardless. A private `_extract_step_report` alias is exposed for internal callers, and a module-level `_STEP_REPORT_RE` is the compiled pattern.
   - `post_step_comment_once(*, repo_owner, repo_name, issue_number, step_num: int, body: str, posted_steps: Set[int], cwd: Path) -> bool`: idempotent trusted post. If `step_num` is already in `posted_steps`, returns `True` immediately without invoking `gh`. Otherwise applies the same redaction + truncation pass used by `_sanitize_comment_body`, shells out to `gh issue comment <issue_number> --repo {owner}/{name} --body <sanitized>` from `cwd`, and on success mutates `posted_steps` in place (adds `step_num`). Returns `True` on success-or-already-posted, `False` on missing `gh` CLI or non-zero exit. Never raises; transient failures are logged via `console.print` and surfaced as `False`. Keyword-only signature — orchestrators must call by keyword to avoid positional drift.
   - `normalize_step_comments_state(raw: Any) -> Set[int]`: coerces the persisted `state["step_comments"]` value into an in-memory `Set[int]`. Accepts (a) `None` / missing key -> empty set; (b) list/tuple/set/frozenset of ints (or strings convertible to int) -> set; (c) the legacy bug-orchestrator dict shape `{"<step>": {"posted": True}, ...}` -> set of int keys whose `posted` *or* `failed_posted` is `True` (so failure-comment posts also count as "already on GitHub"); (d) malformed / unexpected inputs -> empty set. Never raises. `bool` and `float` values are rejected as step numbers (bool is an int subclass, but always a flag in legacy state; float requires composite-key projection at the orchestrator before persistence).
   - **Redaction/truncation pipeline** (used by `post_step_comment_once`): `_SECRET_PATTERNS` covers GitHub PATs (`ghp_`, `github_pat_`, `gho_`/`ghu_`/`ghs_`/`ghr_`), Google AI keys (`AIza`), xAI (`xai-`), OpenAI (`sk-`), Groq (`gsk_`). `_ENV_TOKEN_RE` redacts `GH_TOKEN=...` / `GITHUB_TOKEN=...`. `_BEARER_TOKEN_RE` redacts `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. After redaction, bodies longer than `_COMMENT_MAX_CHARS = 25000` are truncated with a `\n\n…[truncated]` marker; the marker length is reserved so the final body never exceeds the cap. Idempotent and conservative — never raises.
   - **State shape contract**: `state["step_comments"]` persists as a sorted `List[int]` on disk. Orchestrators rehydrate to `Set[int]` via `normalize_step_comments_state` immediately after `load_workflow_state` and write back `sorted(set)` before every `save_workflow_state` call (success path, hard-stop early return, provider-failure abort). For orchestrators with fractional or iterated steps (checkup, test) and orchestrators with cycle loops (e2e_fix), each (step_num, iteration) or (step_num, cycle) tuple is projected to a deterministic non-negative int key before being passed as `step_num=` to the helper — the projection formula is per-orchestrator (documented in their own prompts).
20. **OpenCode Model Resolution**: Resolve the OpenCode model in this order: (1) `OPENCODE_MODEL` env var, kept verbatim including nested slashes like `openrouter/openai/gpt-5.3-codex`; (2) derive a candidate from `llm_model.csv` using PDD's existing model-strength semantics, then translate LiteLLM-oriented IDs via `_translate_to_opencode_model()`. The CSV fallback MUST be auth-aware: build the configured OpenCode provider set from parsed provider credentials in `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json`, parsed usable OpenCode config provider/model entries (`~/.config/opencode/opencode.json`, nearest project `opencode.json`, `OPENCODE_CONFIG`, `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT`), and every provider credential env var represented in `llm_model.csv`; filter candidate rows to providers that are configured before selecting a model. OpenCode config sources contribute a configured provider only when they declare a provider/model path with resolvable auth or explicit local/no-key provider semantics; bare config existence is diagnostic-only. OpenCode agentic runs use `OPENCODE_MODEL` or the auth-aware CSV fallback, not generic direct-prompt model defaults. Required translations include `github_copilot/X -> github-copilot/X`, `gemini/X -> google/X`, bare Anthropic rows like `claude-sonnet-... -> anthropic/claude-sonnet-...`, and bare OpenAI rows like `gpt-5 -> openai/gpt-5`; IDs already in OpenCode `provider/model` form pass through unchanged. If no configured provider can serve the selected model, fail fast with an actionable error telling the user to set `OPENCODE_MODEL=provider/model`, configure the matching provider, or run `opencode models` after authentication. Do not rely on OpenCode default model resolution.
21. **OpenCode Optional Knobs**: Honor `OPENCODE_AGENT` by passing `--agent <name>` and `OPENCODE_VARIANT` by passing `--variant <name>` when set. Omit both flags when unset. `PDD_OPENCODE_MODE` is out of scope for this module version; use `opencode run` only.

% Function Signatures
`get_agent_provider_preference() -> List[str]`
`get_available_agents() -> List[str]`
`run_agentic_task(instruction, cwd, *, verbose=False, quiet=False, label="", timeout=None, max_retries=1, retry_delay=DEFAULT_RETRY_DELAY, deadline=None, use_playwright=False, reasoning_time=None) -> Tuple[bool, str, float, str]`
- Tries providers in preference order. Returns (success, output, cost, provider).
- `use_playwright` enables constrained tools (see section).
- Uses exponential backoff with jitter: `retry_delay * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) + random.uniform(0, retry_delay)`.
- Classifies errors as permanent/transient via `_is_permanent_error()` before retrying.
- Enforces aggregate per-step timeout (`2 * effective_timeout` when no deadline set).
- `reasoning_time` accepts an explicit user-supplied `--time` value forwarded by command modules and is `None` when the command used only the global default.
`detect_control_token(output, token, tail_lines=_SEMANTIC_TAIL_LINES) -> Optional[TokenMatch]`
`classify_step_output(output, expected_tokens, model="gemini/gemini-3-flash") -> Optional[TokenMatch]`
`_codex_auth_failure_message(error_detail: str) -> Optional[str]`
`substitute_template_variables(template, variables) -> str`
`set_agentic_progress(workflow, current_step, total_steps, step_name, completed_steps) -> None`
`clear_agentic_progress() -> None`
`get_and_clear_agentic_interrupt_context() -> Optional[Dict]`
`get_job_deadline() -> Optional[float]`
`post_step_comment(repo_owner, repo_name, issue_number, step_num, total_steps, description, output, cwd, body=None) -> bool` — Issue #964: when `body` is provided (orchestrator extracted a `<step_report>` block from a successful run), strip any leading duplicate `## Step <N>` header from the model body, sanitize via `_sanitize_comment_body`, and wrap in the orchestrator's canonical header. When `body=None`, retain the legacy FAILED fallback template using `output` (used by hard-stop callers in `agentic_change_orchestrator`).
`_extract_step_report(text: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]` — Extracts the LAST `<step_report>...</step_report>` block (DOTALL + case-insensitive). Returns `None` when text is empty or no tagged block is present; otherwise the body is whitespace-stripped. Orchestrators treat `None` as a successful-step fallback case: post a short visible comment that raw output is retained in workflow state, then preserve the raw output in state.
`_sanitize_comment_body(body: Optional[str], max_chars: int = 25_000) -> str` — Redacts known secret formats (GitHub tokens, Google API keys, OpenAI/xAI/Groq keys, `GH_TOKEN=` / `GITHUB_TOKEN=` env, Bearer headers) then truncates to `max_chars`. Truncation reserves room for the `…[truncated]` marker so the returned length never exceeds `max_chars`. Idempotent.
`extract_step_report(text: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]` — Public alias for `_extract_step_report`; orchestrators import this name for clarity at the call site.
`post_step_comment_once(*, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, issue_number: int, step_num: int, body: str, posted_steps: Set[int], cwd: Path) -> bool` — Idempotent trusted post via `gh issue comment`. Returns `True` immediately if `step_num` is already in `posted_steps`; otherwise sanitizes/truncates `body`, posts, and mutates `posted_steps` in place on success. Keyword-only signature. Never raises; logs and returns `False` on failure.
`normalize_step_comments_state(raw: Any) -> Set[int]` — Coerces a persisted `state["step_comments"]` value (list/tuple/set/frozenset/legacy-dict/None/garbage) into a `Set[int]` of step keys. Never raises. Rejects `bool` (legacy flag) and `float` (requires composite-key projection at the orchestrator).
`post_pr_comment(repo_owner, repo_name, pr_number, body, cwd) -> bool`
`post_final_comment(repo_owner, repo_name, issue_number, reason, total_cost, steps_completed, total_steps, cwd) -> bool`
`validate_cached_state(last_completed_step, step_outputs, step_order=None, quiet=False) -> Union[int, float]`
`load_workflow_state(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True) -> Tuple[Optional[Dict], Optional[int]]`
`github_save_state(repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state: Dict, cwd: Path, comment_id: Optional[int] = None, *, dedupe: bool = False) -> Optional[int]`
`save_workflow_state(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state: Dict, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True, github_comment_id: Optional[int] = None, *, dedupe: bool = False) -> Optional[int]` - Must return `None` on GitHub failure (Issue #830). When `dedupe=True` and there is no current GitHub comment id, adopt the newest matching state comment and delete older duplicates before saving.
`clear_workflow_state(cwd: Path, issue_number: int, workflow_type: str, state_dir: Path, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, use_github_state: bool = True) -> None`

% Constants
- `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: 600.0`
- `MIN_VALID_OUTPUT_LENGTH: 50`
- `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES: 3`
- `JOB_TIMEOUT_MARGIN_SECONDS: 120.0`
- `MIN_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: 60.0`
- `MAX_ERROR_SNIPPET_LENGTH: 2000`
- `MAX_ERROR_RESPONSE_NEWLINES: 3` (Issue #1232: newline-count gate for leading-`Error:` false-positive heuristic)

% Token Pricing
`Pricing(input_per_million, output_per_million, cached_input_multiplier)`
- `GEMINI`: flash (0.35/1.05, 0.5), pro (3.50/10.50, 0.5)
- `CODEX`: 1.50/6.00, 0.25
- `ANTHROPIC`: opus (15/75, 0.1), sonnet (3/15, 0.1), haiku (0.8/4, 0.1).
- OpenCode: prefer costs reported by JSONL events; fallback pricing uses the resolved provider/model's existing `llm_model.csv` row rather than a new hardcoded `Pricing` constant.
- Formula: `new_input = max(0, total_input - cached)`. Anthropic: `new_input = total - cache_read - cache_write`. Cache write billed at 1.25x input.

% CLI Discovery
`_find_cli_binary(name, config=None) -> Optional[str]`
`_load_agentic_config() -> Dict`
`_get_cli_diagnostic_info(name) -> str`

% CLI Invocation
Instruction: `"Read the file {prompt_file.name} for instructions. You have full file access to explore and modify files as needed."`
- Anthropic: `[cli_path, "-p", "-", "--dangerously-skip-permissions", "--output-format", "json"]` (pipe via stdin).
- Google: `[cli_path, "<instruction>", "--yolo", "--output-format", "json"]`.
- OpenAI: `[cli_path, "exec", "--sandbox", <mode>, "--json", "-"]` and pipe the full prompt body via stdin. Mode default: `danger-full-access`.
- OpenCode: write the full prompt/instruction body to the same temporary prompt file used by the other providers, then pass only a short directive such as `"Read the file {prompt_file.name} for your full instructions and execute them."` as the trailing message: `[cli_path, "run", "--dir", str(cwd), "--format", "json", "--dangerously-skip-permissions", "--model", <resolved provider/model>, "--", <short read-file directive>]`. Append `--agent <OPENCODE_AGENT>` and `--variant <OPENCODE_VARIANT>` only when those env vars are set. The full generated prompt MUST NOT be passed as a single argv element because large issue prompts can exceed OS argv limits before OpenCode starts. Output is JSONL (one JSON object per line), so parsing must handle JSONL in addition to single-object JSON.

% Playwright Mode (`use_playwright=True`)
- Anthropic: Replaces `--dangerously-skip-permissions` with `--allowedTools`, `"Bash"`, `"Read"`, `"Write"`, `--max-turns", `"30"`.
- Google/OpenAI/OpenCode: Same as standard (no per-tool restrictions).

% GitHub State Persistence
- `_load_gh_paginated_comments`: Parse GitHub issue comments emitted by `gh api --paginate --slurp` by flattening nested page arrays into one `List[Dict]`; also tolerate concatenated JSON page documents from plain `--paginate`.
- `_find_state_comment`: Use `gh api ... --paginate --slurp`. Scans ALL comments and returns the one with the HIGHEST id (most recently written) that contains the workflow state marker. Under the duplicate-marker hazard (two workers both POST a state comment, or a prior run's comment was never fully cleared), returning the highest-id marker ensures a normal resume always loads the most recently written state rather than silently loading an older stale one. Callers that need every matching id (e.g. for sweep-delete) must use `_find_all_state_comments` instead.
- `_find_all_state_comments`: Same paginated/slurped list call, but returns EVERY comment id whose body carries the workflow's state marker (issue #1149). Required for `github_clear_state`'s sweep behavior and for `github_save_state(..., dedupe=True)`. Returns `[]` on any error.
- `github_clear_state`: MUST delete EVERY matching state comment, not just the first one. The legacy single-comment delete left older duplicate markers behind (a possible artifact of a prior race) so a future normal resume would silently pick the stale one. Iterate `_find_all_state_comments` and best-effort delete each; return `True` only if every delete succeeded.
- `github_save_state` (`dedupe: bool = False`, keyword-only): when `dedupe=True` AND `comment_id is None`, the helper first lists every existing state comment via `_find_all_state_comments`. If any exist, it PATCHes the highest-id (most-recent) marker in place with the new state and DELETEs the rest, returning the adopted id; if none exist, it falls through to the normal POST. This closes the clean-restart race where a concurrent worker re-created a state comment in the gap between the pre-clear sweep and the first save. `dedupe=False` (default) preserves the original blind-POST behavior so the extra list-comments cost is opt-in. Concurrent-worker races AFTER the first save (both workers holding valid `github_comment_id`s pointing at different physical comments) are out of scope: the orchestrator's whole-life invariant is "exactly one state comment, the one we know about", and the dedupe path closes only the pre-first-save window.
- `save_workflow_state` (`dedupe: bool = False`, keyword-only): writes local first, then GitHub. If `github_save_state` returns `None`, return `None` (detect divergence). Forwards `dedupe` verbatim to `github_save_state` so caller-side opt-in surfaces all the way down.

% Instructions
1. Implement `_subprocess_run` using `Popen(..., start_new_session=True)`. Catch `TimeoutExpired` and kill process group (`-proc.pid`).
2. `run_agentic_task`: Inject `PDD_USER_FEEDBACK` if present. Use exponential backoff with jitter for retries: `backoff = retry_delay * (2 ** (attempt - 1)) + random.uniform(0, retry_delay)`, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY`. Classify errors via `_is_permanent_error()` before retrying — permanent errors skip retries and move to next provider. **Rate-limit awareness:** when the prior attempt was rate-limited (`_is_rate_limited(last_output) -> bool`, matching HTTP 429 / `"rate limit"` / `"too many requests"` / `"requests per minute"` patterns), raise the next-sleep floor to `RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_FLOOR` (60s) so token-window limits have time to clear instead of burning the retry budget on the same 429. For OpenAI/Codex nonzero exits, `_run_with_provider` must inspect combined stderr/stdout with `_codex_auth_failure_message()` before returning a generic `Exit code ...` string, so stale Codex login failures become actionable `codex login` guidance and remain permanent.
3. Detect single-letter files `C`, `E`, `T` after success; log red warning via `console.print`.
4. `_parse_provider_json` returns `(success, text, cost, actual_model)` (4-tuple — Issue #1376). `actual_model` is the model name extracted via `_extract_provider_model_from_data(provider, data)`: keys of `modelUsage` for anthropic, keys of `stats.models` for google, `data["model"]` (with `data["session"]["model"]` / `data["item"]["model"]` fallback) for openai, and OpenCode `model` / nested session-message-part model fields. Multiple model keys join with `+`. Returns `None` when the JSON does not surface a model. Provider-specific text/cost extraction:
   - Anthropic: `result` or `response`. If `data.get("is_error")` is truthy (Claude Code session failed — auth error, refusal, crash, e.g. `{"is_error": true, "result": "Not logged in - Please run /login"}`), return `(False, text, cost, actual_model)` so the caller treats it as a real failure instead of empty success while still surfacing the model. When `data["api_error_status"]` is present (e.g. `429` for rate-limit envelopes, `400` for credit-exhaustion), prepend `f"HTTP {api_error_status}: "` to the returned text. Without that prefix the rate-limit body — whose `result` is often just "Please go to Plans & Billing..." — would lose its 429 marker before `_classify_permanent_error`/`_is_rate_limited` see it and the weak billing-page hint would misclassify a transient 429 as `billing/credit-exhaustion` (Issue #814).
   - Google: `result`, `response`, or `output`.
   - OpenAI (0.104.0+): `data["item"]["text"]` if `item["type"] == "agent_message"`, else fallback. **NDJSON merge contract:** when `item.completed` carries the agent text and `session.end` / `turn.completed` carries usage and model, the merge must preserve **both** `usage` (for cost) and `model` (for actual-model attribution) on the merged data dict — dropping `model` would re-introduce `model: null` for default-model Codex runs.
   - OpenCode: Parse JSONL line-by-line using a private helper such as `_parse_opencode_jsonl` that returns text, success/failure, summed cost, actual model, and accounting metadata. Concatenate visible `text` event payloads from `part.text` (with top-level `text` / `content` tolerated for compatibility) into the output channel; treat `error` events as failure with their message; ignore `step_start`, `tool_use`, and `step_finish` for text output while still capturing `step_finish.part.cost`, whether any cost field was actually reported (`cost_reported`), and token counters (`input`, `output`, `reasoning`, `cache.read`, `cache.write`) for accounting. Unknown event types are ignored for forward compatibility. If `cost_reported` is false, `_run_with_provider` MUST compute CSV fallback pricing from the resolved OpenCode `provider/model` and either captured token counters or the existing token-estimation path; successful text output with missing cost fields must not return `$0.00` unless the matched CSV row is genuinely free.
5. Support model overrides: `CLAUDE_MODEL`, `GEMINI_MODEL`, `CODEX_MODEL`, `OPENCODE_MODEL`.
6. Reasoning effort: `_run_with_provider` resolves a generic `reasoning_effort` from (a) explicit `reasoning_time` kwarg only when the caller forwarded a user-supplied `--time` (commands gate on `ctx.obj["time_explicit"]` — never pass the `DEFAULT_TIME` fallback) or (b) `PDD_REASONING_EFFORT` env var (validated against `{low, medium, high}`; any other value is ignored to prevent argv poisoning). For Codex, additionally check `CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT` env var first — it accepts `{low, medium, high, xhigh}` (xhigh is Codex-only, used by the cloud worker for GPT-5.4 routing) and takes precedence over the generic `reasoning_effort`. Inject `-c model_reasoning_effort=<level>` **before** the `exec` subcommand — Codex treats `-c` / `--config` as top-level flags only and silently ignores them after `exec`. Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenCode CLIs have no generic PDD reasoning-effort flag today; emit a one-line notice in non-quiet mode rather than silently dropping the signal. OpenCode provider-specific reasoning variants are controlled separately by `OPENCODE_VARIANT`.

===== agentic_common_worktree_python.prompt =====
<pdd-reason>Shared worktree setup, git-scope guards, and agentic output parsing helpers for orchestrators.</pdd-reason>

<pdd-interface>
{
  "type": "module",
  "module": {
    "functions": [
      {"name": "get_git_root", "signature": "(cwd: Path)", "returns": "Optional[Path]"},
      {"name": "worktree_exists", "signature": "(cwd: Path, worktree_path: Path)", "returns": "bool"},
      {"name": "branch_exists", "signature": "(cwd: Path, branch: str)", "returns": "bool"},
      {"name": "remove_worktree", "signature": "(cwd: Path, worktree_path: Path)", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str]"},
      {"name": "delete_branch", "signature": "(cwd: Path, branch: str)", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str]"},
      {"name": "resolve_main_ref", "signature": "(git_root: Path)", "returns": "str"},
      {"name": "setup_worktree", "signature": "(cwd, issue_number, quiet, *, resume_existing, branch_prefix, worktree_prefix)", "returns": "Tuple[Optional[Path], Optional[str]]"},
      {"name": "get_modified_and_untracked", "signature": "(cwd: Path)", "returns": "List[str]"},
      {"name": "check_target_file_unchanged", "signature": "(cwd, target_file, baseline_sha)", "returns": "Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]"},
      {"name": "revert_out_of_scope_changes_with_dirs", "signature": "(cwd, allowed_dirs, allowed_files)", "returns": "List[Path]"},
      {"name": "extract_block_marker", "signature": "(output, name)", "returns": "str"}
    ]
  }
}
</pdd-interface>

<pdd-dependency>agentic_common_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>
<pdd-dependency>git_porcelain_python.prompt</pdd-dependency>

<include>context/python_preamble.prompt</include>

% Goal
Write the `pdd/agentic_common_worktree.py` module — shared worktree setup, git-scope guards, and agentic output parsing helpers used by multiple orchestrators.

% Role & Scope
Reusable git-worktree and scope-guard utilities extracted from `agentic_bug_orchestrator`. Any orchestrator (bug, split, change, etc.) that needs to create worktrees, detect concurrent edits, enforce scope boundaries, or parse multi-line marker blocks from agent output should import from this module instead of reimplementing inline.

All functions are public (no leading underscore) so orchestrators can import them directly.

% Requirements
1. **`get_git_root(cwd: Path) -> Optional[Path]`**: Return repo root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, or `None` on failure.
2. **`worktree_exists(cwd: Path, worktree_path: Path) -> bool`**: Check if `worktree_path` appears in `git worktree list --porcelain` output (run from repo root).
3. **`branch_exists(cwd: Path, branch: str) -> bool`**: Check via `git show-ref --verify refs/heads/{branch}`.
4. **`remove_worktree(cwd: Path, worktree_path: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]`**: Remove via `git worktree remove --force`. Return `(success, error_msg)`.
5. **`delete_branch(cwd: Path, branch: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]`**: Delete via `git branch -D`. Return `(success, error_msg)`.
6. **`resolve_main_ref(git_root: Path) -> str`**: Try `origin/main`, `origin/master`, `main`, `master` in order via `git rev-parse --verify`. Return commit hash of first found, or `"HEAD"` as last resort.
7. **`setup_worktree(cwd, issue_number, quiet, *, resume_existing=False, branch_prefix="fix", worktree_prefix="fix") -> Tuple[Optional[Path], Optional[str]]`**: Create an isolated git worktree at `.pdd/worktrees/{worktree_prefix}-issue-{issue_number}/` on branch `{branch_prefix}/issue-{issue_number}`. Clean up existing worktree/directory and branch before creating. If `resume_existing` is True and branch exists, reuse it (attach with `--force`). Otherwise delete the old branch first. When reusing an undeletable branch, reset to main ref after attaching. Print worktree path unless `quiet`. The `branch_prefix` and `worktree_prefix` kwargs let callers customize naming (e.g. `change` prefix for change workflows, `fix` for bug workflows). Return `(worktree_path, None)` on success, `(None, error_msg)` on failure.
8. **`get_modified_and_untracked(cwd: Path) -> List[str]`**: Return modified tracked files (`git diff --name-only HEAD`) plus untracked files (`git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`).
9. **`check_target_file_unchanged(cwd: Path, target_file: str, baseline_sha: Optional[str] = None) -> Tuple[bool, Optional[str]]`**: Detect concurrent edits. Run `git fetch origin` then `git rev-parse origin/main:{target_file}`. If `baseline_sha` is provided, compare current SHA against it — return `(True, current_sha)` if unchanged, `(False, current_sha)` if changed. If `baseline_sha` is None, just return `(True, current_sha)` to establish the baseline. Return `(True, None)` on git failures (fail-open to avoid blocking workflows).
10. **`revert_out_of_scope_changes_with_dirs(cwd: Path, allowed_dirs: set[str], allowed_files: set[Path]) -> List[Path]`**: Scope guard that detects both tracked changes AND new untracked files via `git status --porcelain=v1 -z -u`, parsed via `pdd.git_porcelain.parse_porcelain_z` so renamed paths surface BOTH the new and old paths as discrete fields (the earlier text-mode `--porcelain` parser kept only the new side, which let an out-of-scope old path escape the scope guard — see issue #1080). For each changed/new file, check if its path starts with any prefix in `allowed_dirs` OR its resolved absolute path is in `allowed_files`. For renames (`R` status), treat the change as out-of-scope if EITHER side is out of scope; unstage both sides with `git reset HEAD -- <new> <old>`, restore the old path via `git checkout HEAD -- <old>`, and delete the new path. For copies (`C` status), the old path is only the source and is not modified; scope and revert decisions are based on the copied destination only, and reverting an out-of-scope copy must unstage/remove the destination without touching the source. Revert other tracked out-of-scope changes via `git checkout HEAD -- <file>`. Remove untracked out-of-scope files via `os.remove`. Return list of reverted/removed paths. Log actions via module logger. Handle timeout and OS errors gracefully.
11. **`extract_block_marker(output: str, name: str) -> str`**: Parse a multi-line block delimited by `BEGIN_{name}` and `END_{name}` markers from agent output. Return the content between markers (stripped), or empty string if markers not found. Case-insensitive marker matching.

% Dependencies
<pdd.agentic_common><include>context/agentic_common_example.py</include></pdd.agentic_common>

% Deliverables
- Code: `pdd/agentic_common_worktree.py`


===== agentic_crash_explore_LLM.prompt =====
You are fixing a crash in a PDD (Prompt-Driven Development) project.
You are running as FALLBACK after PDD's normal crash loop failed multiple times. This loop was only allowed to change the code and/or program file.
The error(s) is likely outside of these files.

## PDD Principle
The PROMPT FILE is the source of truth. Code is a generated artifact.
The PROGRAM FILE calls the code and crashed. Both files may need fixes.

## Files (you have full read/write access)
- Prompt file (THE SPEC): {prompt_path}
- Code file: {code_path}
- Program file: {program_path}
- Project root: {project_root}

## Previous Fix Attempts
The following shows what PDD's normal crash loop already tried.
DO NOT repeat these approaches - try something different.

<previous_attempts>
{previous_attempts}
</previous_attempts>

## Your Task
1. Read the prompt file to understand the intended behavior
2. Read the code and program files
3. Analyze the crash traceback to identify the root cause
4. Explore related files (imports, dependencies, conftest.py) if needed
5. Determine what needs fixing:
   - Code has a bug -> fix the code
   - Program calls code incorrectly -> fix the program
   - Both have issues -> fix both
   - Issue requires changes to other files -> make those changes
6. Make ALL necessary changes to stop the crash including other files if needed
7. Run the program file to verify the fix
8. Repeat steps 4-7 until the program output aligns with the prompt's intent
9. Output a JSON string with the following fields:
   - success: bool
   - message: str
   - cost: float
   - model: str
   - changed_files: list[str]

## Critical Rules
- The prompt file defines what's correct - code should conform to it
- DO NOT repeat approaches from the fix history above
- You MAY modify BOTH the code file AND the program file
- IMPORTANT: Read actual source files before assuming what functions/classes exist
  - Do NOT guess at imports or API names
  - Explore the codebase to understand actual exports

## Common Crash Patterns

### Missing Environment Variables
If the error shows KeyError for an API key/token, or "Set the XXX environment variable":
- This is NOT a code bug. The example needs graceful degradation.
- Fix the PROGRAM FILE to check for the env var with os.environ.get() + sys.exit(0)
- Do NOT hardcode API keys or dummy values.
- Use sys.exit(0) (not sys.exit(1)) so PDD treats it as non-crash.


===== agentic_crash_python.prompt =====
<include>context/python_preamble.prompt</include>

% Goal
Write the pdd/agentic_crash.py module.

% Role & Scope
Agentic fallback for crash command. When LLM-based crash fixes fail,
delegates to a CLI agent with full codebase exploration capability.

Uses explore mode only because:
1. Non-agentic loop already exhausted fixing just the code and/or program file. The error(s) is likely outside of these files.
2. What's needed is exploration, not another fix attempt with the code and/or program file.

IMPORTANT: Crash can fix BOTH the code file AND the program file.

% Entry Point
`run_agentic_crash(prompt_file, code_file, program_file, crash_log_file, *, verbose: bool = False, quiet: bool = False)`

Note: `program_file` is the calling program that crashed.
      `crash_log_file` contains crash traceback + previous fix attempts.

% LLM Prompt Template
Uses `agentic_crash_explore_LLM` (loaded via load_prompt_template).

% Requirements
1. Single-pass: explore mode only
2. Project root is `Path.cwd()` (not derived from prompt file path)
3. Record file mtimes before agent runs, detect changes after; merge with agent-reported changed_files (set union)
7. **Scope Guard**: After `run_agentic_task` returns (before the post-task mtime snapshot), call `_revert_out_of_scope_changes(project_root, {code_path.resolve(), program_path.resolve()})` from `agentic_common`. Import it alongside the other agentic_common imports.
4. Verification after fix:
   - Python: run the program file locally to verify no crash
     - Use `subprocess.DEVNULL` for stdin to prevent hangs on `input()`
     - 120-second timeout to prevent infinite loops/hangs
   - Non-Python: trust the agent's own verification result (the agent already ran the program using language-appropriate tools)
5. Parse the json from the agentic CLI call to get the success, message, cost, model, and changed_files
6. Return 5-tuple: `(success, message, cost, model, changed_files)`

% Dependencies

</module_dependencies>

% Deliverables
- Code: `pdd/agentic_crash.py`

===== agentic_e2e_fix_orchestrator_python.prompt =====
<include>context/python_preamble.prompt</include>
<pdd-reason>Orchestrates the 12-step agentic E2E fix workflow including post-push CI validation, code cleanup, and a final PR-mode checkup gate that re-validates CI when it pushes additional fixes.</pdd-reason>

<pdd-interface>
{
  "type": "module",
  "module": {
    "functions": [
      {"name": "run_agentic_e2e_fix_orchestrator", "signature": "(issue_url, issue_content, repo_owner, repo_name, issue_number, issue_author, issue_title, *, cwd, timeout_adder=0.0, max_cycles=5, resume=True, verbose=False, quiet=False, use_github_state=True, protect_tests=False, ci_retries=3, skip_ci=False, skip_cleanup=False, reasoning_time=None, clean_restart=False)", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str, float, str, List[str]]"},
      {"name": "push_with_retry", "signature": "(cwd: Path, *, remote: str = 'origin', repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, refspec: str = 'HEAD', set_upstream: bool = True, force_with_lease_on_non_fast_forward: bool = True) -> Tuple[bool, str]", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str]", "sideEffects": ["Runs git push <remote> <refspec>; retries with --force-with-lease on non-fast-forward only when force_with_lease_on_non_fast_forward is true", "On auth failure, reads a token from PDD_GH_TOKEN_FILE, then GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, and PDD_GITHUB_TOKEN, rewrites the remote URL to inject x-access-token, retries once, and restores the original URL in finally"]},
      {"name": "_fetch_pr_head_sha", "signature": "(repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, pr_number: int) -> str", "returns": "str", "sideEffects": ["Calls gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{n} via the shared _fetch_pr_metadata helper", "Best-effort: returns empty string on any failure so callers can fail closed"]},
      {"name": "_read_checkup_worktree_head_sha", "signature": "(cwd: Path, pr_number: int) -> str", "returns": "str", "sideEffects": ["Locates .pdd/worktrees/checkup-pr-{N}/ relative to cwd's git root and runs git rev-parse HEAD inside it", "Returns the SHA the checkup last verified or pushed; comparing against the live PR remote SHA is what distinguishes a checkup self-push from an external party advancing the PR during the checkup", "Best-effort: returns empty string when the worktree is missing or the rev-parse fails"]},
      {"name": "_run_final_checkup_on_pr", "signature": "(*, issue_url: str, issue_number: int, repo_owner: str, repo_name: str, cwd: Path, verbose: bool, quiet: bool, timeout_adder: float, use_github_state: bool, reasoning_time: Optional[float], ci_step_template: str, ci_validation_timeout: float) -> Tuple[bool, str, float, str]", "returns": "Tuple[bool, str, float, str]", "sideEffects": ["Snapshots PR remote head SHA before/after run_agentic_checkup", "When pre and post SHAs differ, reads the checkup worktree HEAD via _read_checkup_worktree_head_sha; if it doesn't equal the post-checkup remote SHA, an external push raced the checkup — fail closed without re-validating CI because Step 7 never saw the new code", "When the worktree HEAD matches the post-checkup remote SHA, re-runs run_ci_validation_loop with max_retries=0 and expected_head_sha_override=<post-checkup SHA>", "Fails closed when any of pre-checkup SHA, post-checkup SHA, or checkup-worktree HEAD SHA cannot be fetched — silent success there would re-introduce the post-CI mutation hole closed in PR #1112"]}
    ]
  }
}
</pdd-interface>

<pdd.agentic_e2e_fix_orchestrator>
